title: NA-ULIP
word_text:
Premium Redirection in ULIP: How & When to Do It?
What is the ULIP plan and how does it work?
A Unit Linked Insurance Plan (ULIP) is an investment-oriented life insurance policy which allows insurance protection and market-linked returns. The premiums invested in the plan are invested in market-linked securities. Thereafter, depending on market movements, the value of the invested amount may change. The returns are not guaranteed but ULIPs allow you to create a market-linked corpus, associated with market risks, for your financial goals while enjoying insurance coverage.
The working of a ULIP can be understood through the following points –
When you buy a ULIP, you can choose the premium to be paid (provided it is within the minimum and maximum limits specified by the insurance company).
You can also choose the policy tenure, premium payment tenure and frequency and the investment market-linked fund into which you want to direct your premium.
The premium paid is invested in the chosen fund, post deduction of applicable charges.
The fund has a diversified portfolio of securities which are traded on the financial market. Depending on the performance of the market, the value of the securities rises or falls. Based on the fund performance, the value of the overall portfolio changes and you might earn returns accordingly.
There’s a lock-in period of 5 years after which you may make partial withdrawals from the fund value to meet your financial needs subject to policy terms & conditions
The policy usually runs till the chosen tenure or premature demise of the life insured, whichever is earlier.
ULIPs offer the flexibility of a fund switch wherein you can change the type of investment fund. There is also another feature of premium redirection wherein you can redirect your future premium to another fund of your choice.
In the case of demise, a death benefit is paid while on maturity, the ULIP maturity benefit is paid.
Premium redirection in ULIP
Premium redirection is a flexible feature allowed under ULIPs wherein you can redirect future premiums to another fund. To understand what premium redirection is and how it works, consider the following example –
Suppose you buy a ULIP and invest your premium in Fund A. After a few years, you want to invest the subsequent premiums in Fund B without affecting any change in the existing investments in Fund A. This is where the premium redirection feature would help.
In the initial years, till the premium redirection option is not chosen, your investment would continue to be directed to Fund A which you chose when buying the plan. However, when you choose the premium redirection feature, the premium that you pay would be redirected to another fund that you choose, in this case, Fund B. The premium invested in Fund A would remain unaffected but future premiums would be credited to Fund B. If you choose the premium redirection feature again during the policy tenure, investments in Fund B would stop and the premium would be redirected into another fund that you choose.
Is premium redirection useful?
Premium redirection helps you invest in funds of your choice. If, during the policy tenure, your risk profile or fund preferences change, you can choose the premium redirection feature to redirect the premium to another fund. The feature is helpful because –
It gives you complete control of your investments.
You can change the fund choice whenever you want.
It helps you align your investment with your changing investment strategy.
When you may consider redirecting your premium?
There is no right time to redirect your premium. You can choose the option anytime that you want to, without restrictions. You can choose to redirect your premium when –
You want to invest in a different fund
You want to strategize your investment in different funds at different intervals
You don’t want to change the investment in the initial fund but want future investments in another fund
You want another fund to be the preferred investment avenue for all your premiums and you switch the funds allocated to the initial fund
How to redirect your premium in ULIP?
The process to redirect future premiums in ULIP is a simple one. All you have to do is inform the insurance company of the premium redirection and the fund to which the premium should be redirected to. You can give the instruction through a written request or online intimation. Choose the fund into which you want to redirect the premium and the redirection would be done on renewal.
It may be better to opt for redirection before the renewal due date so that your request can be processed on time.
Benefits of premium redirection in ULIP
Redirecting your premiums has specific benefits. Some of these include the following –
Aligning your investment with your risk profile
If, during the policy tenure, your risk appetite changes and you want to reduce or increase your equity exposure, you can go for premium redirection. The facility allows you to manage your investment and align it with your risk appetite even if it changes over time.
Free of cost
Insurers usually allow a specified number of redirections free of cost. This free redirection facility is allowed every year. If the redirections exceed the specified limit, then a charge may be levied.
No need for frequent switching
When you opt for premium redirection, your premiums get redirected to another fund and keep on being invested in such fund till you don’t choose otherwise. So, if your investment strategy has changed, you don’t have to switch between the funds continuously. You can choose the redirection feature once and the change would be done. Just switch the existing fund to another fund and then you don’t have to worry about frequent switching.
For instance, say you chose Fund A when you bought the ULIP. After 6 years, you want to move to Fund B. You can redirect the subsequent premiums to Fund B and switch the balance in Fund A to Fund B. Thereafter, all your premiums would be redirected to Fund B, not Fund A. Thus, you wouldn’t have to switch your investment every year from Fund A to Fund B.
No tax implication
Premium redirection does not have any taxation impact on earlier premium paid. Customer can claim deduction under Section 80C of the income Tax Act, 1961 for redirected renewal premium paid subject to provisions stated therein. You can change your funds and avoid any tax implications.
Effects / Impact of Premium Redirection in ULIP
When you redirect the premium, your fund preference changes. If you have chosen a fund which belongs to a different asset class (like equity to debt or vice versa), the inherent investment risks and the return potential would change. Moreover, the tax implication on the maturity benefit would also change, if applicable.
Moreover, as mentioned above, insurance companies allow a limited number of premium redirections. If your redirection request exceeds the free limit, the redirection might also incur an additional charge.
Difference between Premium Redirection and Fund Switching?
Most often than not, people may consider fund switching and premium redirection as one and the same. However, this is not the case. Both features are quite different from one another. Here’s how:
Conclusion
When buying a ULIP, understand the concept of premium redirection and how it works. With the flexibility of changing the investment fund, manage your investments effectively so that they keep pace with the changing market dynamics. Look for the number of free premium redirections allowed by the policy. Try and limit your redirections within the free limit to avoid additional charges.
Choose your investment fund, change them when needed and make the most of your investments.
FAQs
How is Premium Redirection beneficial?
Premium redirection may help you change the previously selected investment fund and invest your premium per your changing risk appetite, investment strategy and market movements. It is a hassle-free feature that allows you to take charge of your investments and manage them actively.
Is premium redirection & fund switching different?
Yes, In switching you change the existing fund value from one fund to another. Under redirection, you redirect the future premiums to another fund while the existing fund value remains unaffected.
When is the right time to redirect premium in ULIP?
The right time depends on your investment strategy, risk appetite, market performance and financial goals. You can redirect the premiums anytime that you want to, based on your needs and requirements. There is no specific time for doing so.
